narrative: FY2024-25 shows a split benchmark. SAIL led SOx and NOx with 0.95 kt and 0.8 kt respectively, while Jindal Stainless led PM with 1.6015 kt and remained second on SOx and NOx. Tata’s strongest result was PM: it improved to 8 kt and ranked 2nd, ahead of SAIL and JSW. Tata’s weaker results were SOx and NOx, where it ranked 3rd with 46 kt and 24 kt respectively, materially above SAIL and Jindal. JSW was the laggard in FY2024-25 overall, ranking 4th on SOx, NOx and PM. The key management takeaway for Tata is that dust controls appear to be delivering relative improvement, but gaseous pollutants remain scale- and route-intensive. Tata’s BRSR indicates relevant capex for SOx, NOx and dust reduction, plus real-time monitoring and upgraded air-pollution-control systems; the next step for competitiveness is translating those controls into sustained SOx/NOx reductions while maintaining the PM improvement trajectory.
